6	import { OhMyOpenCodeConfigSchema } from "../config"
7	import * as mcpLoader from "../features/claude-code-mcp-loader"
8	import * as skillLoader from "../features/opencode-skill-loader"
9	import { createSkillContext } from "./skill-context"
10	
11	describe("createSkillContext", () => {
12	  const testDirectory = join(tmpdir(), `skill-context-test-${Date.now()}`)
13	
14	  beforeEach(() => {
15	    mkdirSync(testDirectory, { recursive: true })
16	  })
17	
18	  afterEach(() => {
19	    rmSync(testDirectory, { recursive: true, force: true })
20	  })
21	
22	  it("excludes discovered playwright skill when browser provider is agent-browser", async () => {
23	    // given
24	    const discoveredPlaywrightDir = join(testDirectory, ".claude", "skills", "playwright")
25	    mkdirSync(discoveredPlaywrightDir, { recursive: true })
26	    writeFileSync(
27	      join(discoveredPlaywrightDir, "SKILL.md"),
28	      [
29	        "---",
30	        "name: playwright",
31	        "description: Discovered playwright skill",
32	        "---",
33	        "Discovered playwright body.",
34	        "",
35	      ].join("\n"),
36	    )
37	
38	    const discoverConfigSourceSkillsSpy = spyOn(
39	      skillLoader,
40	      "discoverConfigSourceSkills",
41	    ).mockResolvedValue([])
42	    const discoverUserClaudeSkillsSpy = spyOn(
43	      skillLoader,
44	      "discoverUserClaudeSkills",
45	    ).mockResolvedValue([])
46	    const discoverOpencodeGlobalSkillsSpy = spyOn(
47	      skillLoader,
48	      "discoverOpencodeGlobalSkills",
49	    ).mockResolvedValue([])
50	    const discoverProjectAgentsSkillsSpy = spyOn(
51	      skillLoader,
52	      "discoverProjectAgentsSkills",
53	    ).mockResolvedValue([])
54	    const discoverGlobalAgentsSkillsSpy = spyOn(
55	      skillLoader,
56	      "discoverGlobalAgentsSkills",
57	    ).mockResolvedValue([])
58	    const getSystemMcpServerNamesSpy = spyOn(
59	      mcpLoader,
60	      "getSystemMcpServerNames",
61	    ).mockReturnValue(new Set<string>())
62	
63	    const pluginConfig = OhMyOpenCodeConfigSchema.parse({
64	      browser_automation_engine: { provider: "agent-browser" },
65	    })
66	
67	    try {
68	      // when
69	      const result = await createSkillContext({
70	        directory: testDirectory,
71	        pluginConfig,
72	      })
73	
74	      // then
75	      expect(result.browserProvider).toBe("agent-browser")
76	      expect(result.mergedSkills.some((skill) => skill.name === "agent-browser")).toBe(true)
77	      expect(result.mergedSkills.some((skill) => skill.name === "playwright")).toBe(false)
78	      expect(result.availableSkills.some((skill) => skill.name === "playwright")).toBe(false)

1	import type { AvailableSkill } from "../agents/dynamic-agent-prompt-builder"
2	import type { OhMyOpenCodeConfig } from "../config"
3	import type { BrowserAutomationProvider } from "../config/schema/browser-automation"
4	import type {
5	  LoadedSkill,
6	  SkillScope,
7	} from "../features/opencode-skill-loader/types"
8	
9	import {
10	  discoverConfigSourceSkills,
11	  discoverUserClaudeSkills,
12	  discoverProjectClaudeSkills,
13	  discoverOpencodeGlobalSkills,
14	  discoverOpencodeProjectSkills,
15	  discoverProjectAgentsSkills,
16	  discoverGlobalAgentsSkills,
17	  mergeSkills,
18	} from "../features/opencode-skill-loader"
19	import { createBuiltinSkills } from "../features/builtin-skills"
20	import { getSystemMcpServerNames } from "../features/claude-code-mcp-loader"
21	
22	export type SkillContext = {
23	  mergedSkills: LoadedSkill[]
24	  availableSkills: AvailableSkill[]
25	  browserProvider: BrowserAutomationProvider
26	  disabledSkills: Set<string>
27	}
28	
29	const PROVIDER_GATED_SKILL_NAMES = new Set(["agent-browser", "playwright"])
30	
31	function mapScopeToLocation(scope: SkillScope): AvailableSkill["location"] {
32	  if (scope === "user" || scope === "opencode") return "user"
33	  if (scope === "project" || scope === "opencode-project") return "project"
34	  return "plugin"
35	}
36	
37	function filterProviderGatedSkills(
38	  skills: LoadedSkill[],
39	  browserProvider: BrowserAutomationProvider,
40	): LoadedSkill[] {
41	  return skills.filter((skill) => {
42	    if (!PROVIDER_GATED_SKILL_NAMES.has(skill.name)) {
43	      return true
44	    }
45	
46	    return skill.name === browserProvider
47	  })
48	}
49	
50	export async function createSkillContext(args: {
51	  directory: string
52	  pluginConfig: OhMyOpenCodeConfig
53	}): Promise<SkillContext> {
54	  const { directory, pluginConfig } = args
55	
56	  const browserProvider: BrowserAutomationProvider =
57	    pluginConfig.browser_automation_engine?.provider ?? "playwright"
58	
59	  const disabledSkills = new Set<string>(pluginConfig.disabled_skills ?? [])
60	  const systemMcpNames = getSystemMcpServerNames()
61	
62	  const builtinSkills = createBuiltinSkills({
63	    browserProvider,
64	    disabledSkills,
65	  }).filter((skill) => {
66	    if (skill.mcpConfig) {
67	      for (const mcpName of Object.keys(skill.mcpConfig)) {
68	        if (systemMcpNames.has(mcpName)) return false
69	      }
70	    }
71	    return true
72	  })
73	
74	  const includeClaudeSkills = pluginConfig.claude_code?.skills !== false
75	  const [configSourceSkills, userSkills, globalSkills, projectSkills, opencodeProjectSkills, agentsProjectSkills, agentsGlobalSkills] =
76	    await Promise.all([
77	      discoverConfigSourceSkills({
78	        config: pluginConfig.skills,
79	        configDir: directory,
80	      }),
81	      includeClaudeSkills ? discoverUserClaudeSkills() : Promise.resolve([]),
82	      discoverOpencodeGlobalSkills(),
83	      includeClaudeSkills ? discoverProjectClaudeSkills(directory) : Promise.resolve([]),
84	      discoverOpencodeProjectSkills(directory),
85	      discoverProjectAgentsSkills(directory),
86	      discoverGlobalAgentsSkills(),
87	    ])
88	
89	  const filteredConfigSourceSkills = filterProviderGatedSkills(
90	    configSourceSkills,
91	    browserProvider,
92	  )
93	  const filteredUserSkills = filterProviderGatedSkills(userSkills, browserProvider)
94	  const filteredGlobalSkills = filterProviderGatedSkills(globalSkills, browserProvider)
95	  const filteredProjectSkills = filterProviderGatedSkills(projectSkills, browserProvider)
96	  const filteredOpencodeProjectSkills = filterProviderGatedSkills(
97	    opencodeProjectSkills,
98	    browserProvider,
99	  )
100	  const filteredAgentsProjectSkills = filterProviderGatedSkills(
101	    agentsProjectSkills,
102	    browserProvider,
103	  )
104	  const filteredAgentsGlobalSkills = filterProviderGatedSkills(
105	    agentsGlobalSkills,
106	    browserProvider,
107	  )
108	
109	  const mergedSkills = mergeSkills(
110	    builtinSkills,
111	    pluginConfig.skills,
112	    filteredConfigSourceSkills,
113	    [...filteredUserSkills, ...filteredAgentsGlobalSkills],
114	    filteredGlobalSkills,
115	    [...filteredProjectSkills, ...filteredAgentsProjectSkills],
116	    filteredOpencodeProjectSkills,
117	    { configDir: directory },
118	  )
119	
120	  const availableSkills: AvailableSkill[] = mergedSkills.map((skill) => ({
121	    name: skill.name,
122	    description: skill.definition.description ?? "",
123	    location: mapScopeToLocation(skill.scope),
124	  }))
125	
126	  return {
127	    mergedSkills,
128	    availableSkills,
129	    browserProvider,
130	    disabledSkills,
131	  }
132	}

import type { CommandDefinition } from "../claude-code-command-loader/types"
import type { SkillMcpConfig } from "../skill-mcp-manager/types"

export type SkillScope = "builtin" | "config" | "user" | "project" | "opencode" | "opencode-project"

export interface SkillMetadata {
  name?: string
  description?: string
  model?: string
  "argument-hint"?: string
  agent?: string
  subtask?: boolean
  license?: string
  compatibility?: string
  metadata?: Record<string, string>
  "allowed-tools"?: string | string[]
  mcp?: SkillMcpConfig
}

export interface LazyContentLoader {
  loaded: boolean
  content?: string
  load: () => Promise<string>
}

export interface LoadedSkill {
  name: string
  path?: string
  resolvedPath?: string
  definition: CommandDefinition
  scope: SkillScope
  license?: string
  compatibility?: string
  metadata?: Record<string, string>
  allowedTools?: string[]
  mcpConfig?: SkillMcpConfig
  lazyContent?: LazyContentLoader
}

65	export interface DiscoverSkillsOptions {
66	  includeClaudeCodePaths?: boolean
67	  directory?: string
68	}

70	export async function discoverAllSkills(directory?: string): Promise<LoadedSkill[]> {
71	  const [opencodeProjectSkills, opencodeGlobalSkills, projectSkills, userSkills, agentsProjectSkills, agentsGlobalSkills] =
72	    await Promise.all([
73	      discoverOpencodeProjectSkills(directory),
74	      discoverOpencodeGlobalSkills(),
75	      discoverProjectClaudeSkills(directory),
76	      discoverUserClaudeSkills(),
77	      discoverProjectAgentsSkills(directory),
78	      discoverGlobalAgentsSkills(),
79	    ])
80	
81	  // Priority: opencode-project > opencode > project (.claude + .agents) > user (.claude + .agents)
82	  return deduplicateSkillsByName([
83	    ...opencodeProjectSkills,
84	    ...opencodeGlobalSkills,
85	    ...projectSkills,
86	    ...agentsProjectSkills,
87	    ...userSkills,
88	    ...agentsGlobalSkills,
89	  ])
90	}

95	  const [opencodeProjectSkills, opencodeGlobalSkills] = await Promise.all([
96	    discoverOpencodeProjectSkills(directory),
97	    discoverOpencodeGlobalSkills(),
98	  ])

100	  if (!includeClaudeCodePaths) {

105	  const [projectSkills, userSkills, agentsProjectSkills, agentsGlobalSkills] = await Promise.all([

110	  ])
111	
112	  // Priority: opencode-project > opencode > project (.claude + .agents) > user (.claude + .agents)
113	  return deduplicateSkillsByName([
114	    ...opencodeProjectSkills,
115	    ...opencodeGlobalSkills,
116	    ...projectSkills,
117	    ...agentsProjectSkills,
118	    ...userSkills,
119	    ...agentsGlobalSkills,
120	  ])
121	}
122	
123	export async function getSkillByName(name: string, options: DiscoverSkillsOptions = {}): Promise<LoadedSkill | undefined> {
124	  const skills = await discoverSkills(options)

1	/// <reference types="bun-types" />
2	
3	import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from "bun:test"
4	import { join } from "node:path"
5	import { tmpdir } from "node:os"
6	import { resolveSkillContent, resolveMultipleSkills, resolveSkillContentAsync, resolveMultipleSkillsAsync } from "./skill-content"
7	
8	let originalEnv: Record<string, string | undefined>
9	let testConfigDir: string
10	
11	beforeEach(() => {
12		originalEnv = {
13			CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR: process.env.CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR,
14			OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR: process.env.OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR,
15		}
16		const unique = `skill-content-test-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(16).slice(2)}`
17		testConfigDir = join(tmpdir(), unique)
18		process.env.CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR = testConfigDir
19		process.env.OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR = testConfigDir
20	})
21	
22	afterEach(() => {
23		for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(originalEnv)) {
24			if (value !== undefined) {
25				process.env[key] = value
26			} else {
27				delete process.env[key]
28			}
29		}
30	})
31	
32	describe("resolveSkillContent", () => {
33		it("should return template for existing skill", () => {
34			// given: builtin skills with 'frontend-ui-ux' skill
35			// when: resolving content for 'frontend-ui-ux'
36			const result = resolveSkillContent("frontend-ui-ux")
37	
38			// then: returns template string
39			expect(result).not.toBeNull()
40			expect(typeof result).toBe("string")
41			expect(result).toContain("Role: Designer-Turned-Developer")
42		})
43	
44		it("should return template for 'playwright' skill", () => {
45			// given: builtin skills with 'playwright' skill
46			// when: resolving content for 'playwright'
47			const result = resolveSkillContent("playwright")
48	
49			// then: returns template string
50			expect(result).not.toBeNull()
51			expect(typeof result).toBe("string")
52			expect(result).toContain("Playwright Browser Automation")
53		})
54	
55		it("should return null for non-existent skill", () => {
56			// given: builtin skills without 'nonexistent' skill
57			// when: resolving content for 'nonexistent'
58			const result = resolveSkillContent("nonexistent")
59	
60			// then: returns null
61			expect(result).toBeNull()
62		})
63	
64		it("should return null for disabled skill", () => {
65			// given: frontend-ui-ux skill disabled
66			const options = { disabledSkills: new Set(["frontend-ui-ux"]) }
67	
68			// when: resolving content for disabled skill
69			const result = resolveSkillContent("frontend-ui-ux", options)
70	
71			// then: returns null
72			expect(result).toBeNull()
73		})
74	})
75	
76	describe("resolveMultipleSkills", () => {
77		it("should resolve all existing skills", () => {
78			// given: list of existing skill names
79			const skillNames = ["frontend-ui-ux", "playwright"]
80	
81			// when: resolving multiple skills
82			const result = resolveMultipleSkills(skillNames)
83	
84			// then: all skills resolved, none not found
85			expect(result.resolved.size).toBe(2)
86			expect(result.notFound).toEqual([])
87			expect(result.resolved.get("frontend-ui-ux")).toContain("Designer-Turned-Developer")
88			expect(result.resolved.get("playwright")).toContain("Playwright Browser Automation")
89		})
90	
91		it("should handle partial success - some skills not found", () => {
92			// given: list with existing and non-existing skills
93			const skillNames = ["frontend-ui-ux", "nonexistent", "playwright", "another-missing"]
94	
95			// when: resolving multiple skills
96			const result = resolveMultipleSkills(skillNames)
97	
98			// then: resolves existing skills, lists not found skills
99			expect(result.resolved.size).toBe(2)
100			expect(result.notFound).toEqual(["nonexistent", "another-missing"])
101			expect(result.resolved.get("frontend-ui-ux")).toContain("Designer-Turned-Developer")
102			expect(result.resolved.get("playwright")).toContain("Playwright Browser Automation")
103		})
104	
105		it("should handle empty array", () => {
106			// given: empty skill names list
107			const skillNames: string[] = []
108	
109			// when: resolving multiple skills
110			const result = resolveMultipleSkills(skillNames)
111	
112			// then: returns empty resolved and notFound
113			expect(result.resolved.size).toBe(0)
114			expect(result.notFound).toEqual([])
115		})
116	
117		it("should handle all skills not found", () => {
118			// given: list of non-existing skills
119			const skillNames = ["skill-one", "skill-two", "skill-three"]
120	
121			// when: resolving multiple skills
122			const result = resolveMultipleSkills(skillNames)
123	
124			// then: no skills resolved, all in notFound
125			expect(result.resolved.size).toBe(0)
126			expect(result.notFound).toEqual(["skill-one", "skill-two", "skill-three"])
127		})
128	
129		it("should treat disabled skills as not found", () => {
130			// #given: frontend-ui-ux disabled, playwright not disabled
131			const skillNames = ["frontend-ui-ux", "playwright"]
132			const options = { disabledSkills: new Set(["frontend-ui-ux"]) }
133	
134			// #when: resolving multiple skills with disabled one
135			const result = resolveMultipleSkills(skillNames, options)
136	
137			// #then: frontend-ui-ux in notFound, playwright resolved
138			expect(result.resolved.size).toBe(1)
139			expect(result.resolved.has("playwright")).toBe(true)
140			expect(result.notFound).toEqual(["frontend-ui-ux"])
141		})
142	
143		it("should preserve skill order in resolved map", () => {
144			// given: list of skill names in specific order
145			const skillNames = ["playwright", "frontend-ui-ux"]
146	
147			// when: resolving multiple skills
148			const result = resolveMultipleSkills(skillNames)
149	
150			// then: map contains skills with expected keys
151			expect(result.resolved.has("playwright")).toBe(true)
152			expect(result.resolved.has("frontend-ui-ux")).toBe(true)
153			expect(result.resolved.size).toBe(2)
154		})
155	})
156	
157	describe("resolveSkillContentAsync", () => {
158		it("should return template for builtin skill async", async () => {
159			// given: builtin skill 'frontend-ui-ux'
160			// when: resolving content async
161			const options = { disabledSkills: new Set(["frontend-ui-ux"]) }
162			const result = await resolveSkillContentAsync("git-master", options)
163	
164			// then: returns template string
165			expect(result).not.toBeNull()
166			expect(typeof result).toBe("string")
167			expect(result).toContain("Git Master Agent")

169	
170		it("should return null for disabled skill async", async () => {
171			// given: frontend-ui-ux disabled

11	export async function loadSkillFromPath(options: {
12	  skillPath: string
13	  resolvedPath: string
14	  defaultName: string
15	  scope: SkillScope
16	  namePrefix?: string
17	}): Promise<LoadedSkill | null> {
18	  const namePrefix = options.namePrefix ?? ""
19	
20	  try {
21	    const content = await fs.readFile(options.skillPath, "utf-8")
22	    const { data, body } = parseFrontmatter<SkillMetadata>(content)
23	
24	    const frontmatterMcp = parseSkillMcpConfigFromFrontmatter(content)
25	    const mcpJsonMcp = await loadMcpJsonFromDir(options.resolvedPath)
26	    const mcpConfig = mcpJsonMcp || frontmatterMcp
27	
28	    const baseName = data.name || options.defaultName
29	    const skillName = namePrefix ? `${namePrefix}/${baseName}` : baseName
30	    const originalDescription = data.description || ""
31	    const isOpencodeSource = options.scope === "opencode" || options.scope === "opencode-project"
32	    const formattedDescription = `(${options.scope} - Skill) ${originalDescription}`
33	
34	    const resolvedBody = resolveSkillPathReferences(body.trim(), options.resolvedPath)
35	    const templateContent = `<skill-instruction>\nBase directory for this skill: ${options.resolvedPath}/\nFile references (@path) in this skill are relative to this directory.\n\n${resolvedBody}\n</skill-instruction>\n\n<user-request>\n$ARGUMENTS\n</user-request>`
36	
37	    const eagerLoader: LazyContentLoader = {
38	      loaded: true,
39	      content: templateContent,
40	      load: async () => templateContent,
41	    }
42	
43	    const definition: CommandDefinition = {
44	      name: skillName,
45	      description: formattedDescription,
46	      template: templateContent,
47	      model: sanitizeModelField(data.model, isOpencodeSource ? "opencode" : "claude-code"),
48	      agent: data.agent,
49	      subtask: data.subtask,
50	      argumentHint: data["argument-hint"],
51	    }
52	
53	    return {
54	      name: skillName,
55	      path: options.skillPath,
56	      resolvedPath: options.resolvedPath,
57	      definition,
58	      scope: options.scope,
59	      license: data.license,
60	      compatibility: data.compatibility,
61	      metadata: data.metadata,
62	      allowedTools: parseAllowedTools(data["allowed-tools"]),
63	      mcpConfig,
64	      lazyContent: eagerLoader,
65	    }
66	  } catch {
67	    return null
68	  }
69	}

1	import { createBuiltinSkills } from "../builtin-skills/skills"
2	import { discoverSkills } from "./loader"
3	import type { LoadedSkill } from "./types"
4	import type { SkillResolutionOptions } from "./skill-resolution-options"
5	
6	const cachedSkillsByProvider = new Map<string, LoadedSkill[]>()
7	
8	export function clearSkillCache(): void {
9		cachedSkillsByProvider.clear()
10	}
11	
12	export async function getAllSkills(options?: SkillResolutionOptions): Promise<LoadedSkill[]> {
13		const cacheKey = options?.browserProvider ?? "playwright"
14		const hasDisabledSkills = options?.disabledSkills && options.disabledSkills.size > 0
15	
16		// Skip cache if disabledSkills is provided (varies between calls)
17		if (!hasDisabledSkills) {
18			const cached = cachedSkillsByProvider.get(cacheKey)
19			if (cached) return cached
20		}
21	
22		const [discoveredSkills, builtinSkillDefinitions] = await Promise.all([
23			discoverSkills({ includeClaudeCodePaths: true, directory: options?.directory }),
24			Promise.resolve(
25				createBuiltinSkills({
26					browserProvider: options?.browserProvider,
27					disabledSkills: options?.disabledSkills,
28				})
29			),
30		])
31	
32		const builtinSkillsAsLoaded: LoadedSkill[] = builtinSkillDefinitions.map((skill) => ({
33			name: skill.name,
34			definition: {
35				name: skill.name,
36				description: skill.description,
37				template: skill.template,
38				model: skill.model,
39				agent: skill.agent,
40				subtask: skill.subtask,
41			},
42			scope: "builtin" as const,
43			license: skill.license,
44			compatibility: skill.compatibility,
45			metadata: skill.metadata as Record<string, string> | undefined,
46			allowedTools: skill.allowedTools,
47			mcpConfig: skill.mcpConfig,
48		}))
49	
50		// Provider-gated skill names that should be filtered based on browserProvider
51		const providerGatedSkillNames = new Set(["agent-browser", "playwright"])
52		const browserProvider = options?.browserProvider ?? "playwright"
53	
54		// Filter discovered skills to exclude provider-gated names that don't match the selected provider
55		const filteredDiscoveredSkills = discoveredSkills.filter((skill) => {
56			if (!providerGatedSkillNames.has(skill.name)) {
57				return true
58			}
59			// For provider-gated skills, only include if it matches the selected provider
60			return skill.name === browserProvider
61		})
62	
63		const discoveredNames = new Set(filteredDiscoveredSkills.map((skill) => skill.name))
64		const uniqueBuiltins = builtinSkillsAsLoaded.filter((skill) => !discoveredNames.has(skill.name))
65	
66		let allSkills = [...filteredDiscoveredSkills, ...uniqueBuiltins]
67	
68		// Filter discovered skills by disabledSkills (builtin skills are already filtered by createBuiltinSkills)
69		if (hasDisabledSkills) {
70			allSkills = allSkills.filter((skill) => !options!.disabledSkills!.has(skill.name))
71		} else {
72			cachedSkillsByProvider.set(cacheKey, allSkills)
73		}
74	
75		return allSkills
76	}

import type { LoadedSkill } from "../types"
import type { SkillDefinition } from "../../../config/schema"
import { deepMerge } from "../../../shared/deep-merge"

export function mergeSkillDefinitions(base: LoadedSkill, patch: SkillDefinition): LoadedSkill {
  const mergedMetadata = base.metadata || patch.metadata
    ? deepMerge(base.metadata || {}, (patch.metadata as Record<string, string>) || {})
    : undefined

  const mergedTools = base.allowedTools || patch["allowed-tools"]
    ? [...(base.allowedTools || []), ...(patch["allowed-tools"] || [])]
    : undefined

  const description = patch.description || base.definition.description?.replace(/^\([^)]+\) /, "")

  return {
    ...base,
    definition: {
      ...base.definition,
      description: `(${base.scope} - Skill) ${description}`,
      model: patch.model || base.definition.model,
      agent: patch.agent || base.definition.agent,
      subtask: patch.subtask ?? base.definition.subtask,
      argumentHint: patch["argument-hint"] || base.definition.argumentHint,
    },
    license: patch.license || base.license,
    compatibility: patch.compatibility || base.compatibility,
    metadata: mergedMetadata as Record<string, string> | undefined,
    allowedTools: mergedTools ? [...new Set(mergedTools)] : undefined,
  }
}

12	export function loadPluginSkillsAsCommands(
13	  plugins: LoadedPlugin[],
14	): Record<string, CommandDefinition> {
15	  const skills: Record<string, CommandDefinition> = {}
16	
17	  for (const plugin of plugins) {
18	    if (!plugin.skillsDir || !existsSync(plugin.skillsDir)) continue
19	
20	    const entries = readdirSync(plugin.skillsDir, { withFileTypes: true })
21	
22	    for (const entry of entries) {
23	      if (entry.name.startsWith(".")) continue
24	
25	      const skillPath = join(plugin.skillsDir, entry.name)
26	      if (!entry.isDirectory() && !entry.isSymbolicLink()) continue
27	
28	      const resolvedPath = resolveSymlink(skillPath)
29	      const skillMdPath = join(resolvedPath, "SKILL.md")
30	      if (!existsSync(skillMdPath)) continue
31	
32	      try {
33	        const content = readFileSync(skillMdPath, "utf-8")
34	        const { data, body } = parseFrontmatter<SkillMetadata>(content)
35	
36	        const skillName = data.name || entry.name
37	        const namespacedName = `${plugin.name}:${skillName}`
38	        const originalDescription = data.description || ""
39	        const formattedDescription = `(plugin: ${plugin.name} - Skill) ${originalDescription}`
40	
41	        const resolvedBody = resolveSkillPathReferences(body.trim(), resolvedPath)
42	        const wrappedTemplate = `<skill-instruction>\nBase directory for this skill: ${resolvedPath}/\nFile references (@path) in this skill are relative to this directory.\n\n${resolvedBody}\n</skill-instruction>\n\n<user-request>\n$ARGUMENTS\n</user-request>`
43	
44	        const definition = {
45	          name: namespacedName,
46	          description: formattedDescription,
47	          template: wrappedTemplate,
48	          model: sanitizeModelField(data.model),
49	        }
50	
51	        const { name: _name, ...openCodeCompatible } = definition
52	        skills[namespacedName] = openCodeCompatible as CommandDefinition
53	
54	        log(`Loaded plugin skill: ${namespacedName}`, { path: resolvedPath })
55	      } catch (error) {
56	        log(`Failed to load plugin skill: ${skillPath}`, error)
57	      }
58	    }

1	import { promises as fs } from "fs"
2	import { join } from "path"
3	import { resolveSymlinkAsync, isMarkdownFile } from "../../shared/file-utils"
4	import type { LoadedSkill, SkillScope } from "./types"
5	import { inferSkillNameFromFileName, loadSkillFromPath } from "./loaded-skill-from-path"
6	
7	export async function loadSkillsFromDir(options: {
8	  skillsDir: string
9	  scope: SkillScope
10	  namePrefix?: string
11	  depth?: number
12	  maxDepth?: number
13	}): Promise<LoadedSkill[]> {
14	  const namePrefix = options.namePrefix ?? ""
15	  const depth = options.depth ?? 0
16	  const maxDepth = options.maxDepth ?? 2
17	
18	  const entries = await fs.readdir(options.skillsDir, { withFileTypes: true }).catch(() => [])
19	  const skillMap = new Map<string, LoadedSkill>()
20	
21	  const directories = entries.filter(
22	    (entry) => !entry.name.startsWith(".") && (entry.isDirectory() || entry.isSymbolicLink())
23	  )
24	  const files = entries.filter(
25	    (entry) =>
26	      !entry.name.startsWith(".") &&
27	      !entry.isDirectory() &&
28	      !entry.isSymbolicLink() &&
29	      isMarkdownFile(entry)
30	  )
31	
32	  for (const entry of directories) {
33	    const entryPath = join(options.skillsDir, entry.name)
34	    const resolvedPath = await resolveSymlinkAsync(entryPath)
35	    const dirName = entry.name
36	
37	    const skillMdPath = join(resolvedPath, "SKILL.md")
38	    try {
39	      await fs.access(skillMdPath)
40	      const skill = await loadSkillFromPath({
41	        skillPath: skillMdPath,
42	        resolvedPath,
43	        defaultName: dirName,
44	        scope: options.scope,
45	        namePrefix,
46	      })
47	      if (skill && !skillMap.has(skill.name)) {
48	        skillMap.set(skill.name, skill)
49	      }
50	      continue
51	    } catch {
52	      // no SKILL.md
53	    }
54	
55	    const namedSkillMdPath = join(resolvedPath, `${dirName}.md`)
56	    try {
57	      await fs.access(namedSkillMdPath)
58	      const skill = await loadSkillFromPath({
59	        skillPath: namedSkillMdPath,
60	        resolvedPath,
61	        defaultName: dirName,
62	        scope: options.scope,
63	        namePrefix,
64	      })
65	      if (skill && !skillMap.has(skill.name)) {
66	        skillMap.set(skill.name, skill)
67	      }
68	      continue
69	    } catch {
70	      // no named md
71	    }
72	
73	    if (depth < maxDepth) {
74	      const newPrefix = namePrefix ? `${namePrefix}/${dirName}` : dirName
75	      const nestedSkills = await loadSkillsFromDir({
76	        skillsDir: resolvedPath,
77	        scope: options.scope,
78	        namePrefix: newPrefix,
79	        depth: depth + 1,
80	        maxDepth,
81	      })
82	      for (const nestedSkill of nestedSkills) {
83	        if (!skillMap.has(nestedSkill.name)) {
84	          skillMap.set(nestedSkill.name, nestedSkill)
85	        }
86	      }
87	    }
88	  }
89	
90	  for (const entry of files) {
91	    const entryPath = join(options.skillsDir, entry.name)
92	    const baseName = inferSkillNameFromFileName(entryPath)
93	    const skill = await loadSkillFromPath({
94	      skillPath: entryPath,
95	      resolvedPath: options.skillsDir,
96	      defaultName: baseName,
97	      scope: options.scope,
98	      namePrefix,
99	    })
100	    if (skill && !skillMap.has(skill.name)) {
101	      skillMap.set(skill.name, skill)
102	    }
103	  }
104	
105	  return Array.from(skillMap.values())
106	}

6	function mapScopeToLocation(scope: SkillScope): AvailableSkill["location"] {
7	  if (scope === "user" || scope === "opencode") return "user"
8	  if (scope === "project" || scope === "opencode-project") return "project"
9	  return "plugin"
10	}

12	export function buildAvailableSkills(
13	  discoveredSkills: LoadedSkill[],
14	  browserProvider?: BrowserAutomationProvider,
15	  disabledSkills?: Set<string>
16	): AvailableSkill[] {
17	  const builtinSkills = createBuiltinSkills({ browserProvider, disabledSkills })
18	  const builtinSkillNames = new Set(builtinSkills.map(s => s.name))
19	
20	  const builtinAvailable: AvailableSkill[] = builtinSkills.map((skill) => ({
21	    name: skill.name,
22	    description: skill.description,
23	    location: "plugin" as const,
24	  }))
25	
26	  const discoveredAvailable: AvailableSkill[] = discoveredSkills
27	    .filter(s => !builtinSkillNames.has(s.name) && !disabledSkills?.has(s.name))
28	    .map((skill) => ({
29	      name: skill.name,
30	      description: skill.definition.description ?? "",
31	      location: mapScopeToLocation(skill.scope),
32	    }))
33	
34	  return [...builtinAvailable, ...discoveredAvailable]
35	}

26	export function categorizeTools(toolNames: string[]): AvailableTool[] {
27	  return toolNames.map((name) => {
28	    let category: AvailableTool["category"] = "other"
29	    if (name.startsWith("lsp_")) {
30	      category = "lsp"
31	    } else if (name.startsWith("ast_grep")) {
32	      category = "ast"
33	    } else if (name === "grep" || name === "glob") {
34	      category = "search"
35	    } else if (name.startsWith("session_")) {
36	      category = "session"
37	    } else if (name === "skill") {
38	      category = "command"
39	    }
40	    return { name, category }
41	  })
42	}

44	function formatToolsForPrompt(tools: AvailableTool[]): string {
45	  const lspTools = tools.filter((t) => t.category === "lsp")
46	  const astTools = tools.filter((t) => t.category === "ast")
47	  const searchTools = tools.filter((t) => t.category === "search")
48	
49	  const parts: string[] = []
50	
51	  if (searchTools.length > 0) {
52	    parts.push(...searchTools.map((t) => `\`${t.name}\``))
53	  }
54	
55	  if (lspTools.length > 0) {
56	    parts.push("`lsp_*`")
57	  }
58	
59	  if (astTools.length > 0) {
60	    parts.push("`ast_grep`")
61	  }
62	
63	  return parts.join(", ")
64	}

79	export function buildToolSelectionTable(
80	  agents: AvailableAgent[],
81	  tools: AvailableTool[] = [],
82	  _skills: AvailableSkill[] = []
83	): string {
84	  const rows: string[] = [
85	    "### Tool & Agent Selection:",

88	
89	  if (tools.length > 0) {
90	    const toolsDisplay = formatToolsForPrompt(tools)

93	
94	  const costOrder = { FREE: 0, CHEAP: 1, EXPENSIVE: 2 }
95	  const sortedAgents = [...agents]
96	    .filter((a) => a.metadata.category !== "utility")
97	    .sort((a, b) => costOrder[a.metadata.cost] - costOrder[b.metadata.cost])

99	  for (const agent of sortedAgents) {

102	  }
103	
104	  rows.push("")
105	  rows.push("**Default flow**: explore/librarian (background) + tools → oracle (if required)")
106	
107	  return rows.join("\n")

203	  } else {

209	**task() combines categories and skills for optimal task execution.**

212	
213	Each category is configured with a model optimized for that domain. Read the description to understand when to use it.
214	
215	${categoryRows.join("\n")}
216	
217	${skillsSection}

244	  load_skills=["skill-1", "skill-2"],  // Include ALL relevant skills — ESPECIALLY user-installed ones

282	export function buildOracleSection(agents: AvailableAgent[]): string {
283	  const oracleAgent = agents.find((a) => a.name === "oracle")
284	  if (!oracleAgent) return ""
285	
286	  const useWhen = oracleAgent.metadata.useWhen || []
287	  const avoidWhen = oracleAgent.metadata.avoidWhen || []
288	
289	  return `<Oracle_Usage>
290	## Oracle — Read-Only High-IQ Consultant
291	
292	Oracle is a read-only, expensive, high-quality reasoning model for debugging and architecture. Consultation only.
293	
294	### WHEN to Consult (Oracle FIRST, then implement):
295	
296	${useWhen.map((w) => `- ${w}`).join("\n")}
297	
298	### WHEN NOT to Consult:
299	
300	${avoidWhen.map((w) => `- ${w}`).join("\n")}
301	
302	### Usage Pattern:
303	Briefly announce "Consulting Oracle for [reason]" before invocation.
304	
305	**Exception**: This is the ONLY case where you announce before acting. For all other work, start immediately without status updates.
306	
307	### Oracle Background Task Policy:
308	
309	**Collect Oracle results before your final answer. No exceptions.**
310	
311	**Oracle-dependent implementation is BLOCKED until Oracle finishes.**
312	
313	- If you asked Oracle for architecture/debugging direction that affects the fix, do not implement before Oracle result arrives.
314	- While waiting, only do non-overlapping prep work. Never ship implementation decisions Oracle was asked to decide.
315	- Never "time out and continue anyway" for Oracle-dependent tasks.
316	
317	- Oracle takes minutes. When done with your own work: **end your response** — wait for the \`<system-reminder>\`.
318	- Do NOT poll \`background_output\` on a running Oracle. The notification will come.
319	- Never cancel Oracle.
320	</Oracle_Usage>`
321	}

372	export function buildNonClaudePlannerSection(model: string): string {
373	  const isNonClaude = !model.toLowerCase().includes('claude')
374	  if (!isNonClaude) return ""
375	
376	  return `### Plan Agent Dependency (Non-Claude)
377	
378	Multi-step task? **ALWAYS consult Plan Agent first.** Do NOT start implementation without a plan.
379	
380	- Single-file fix or trivial change → proceed directly
381	- Anything else (2+ steps, unclear scope, architecture) → \`task(subagent_type="plan", ...)\` FIRST
382	- Use \`session_id\` to resume the same Plan Agent — ask follow-up questions aggressively
383	- If ANY part of the task is ambiguous, ask Plan Agent before guessing
384	
385	Plan Agent returns a structured work breakdown with parallel execution opportunities. Follow it.`
386	}

388	export function buildParallelDelegationSection(model: string, categories: AvailableCategory[]): string {
389	  const isNonClaude = !model.toLowerCase().includes('claude')
390	  const hasDelegationCategory = categories.some(c => c.name === 'deep' || c.name === 'unspecified-high')
391	
392	  if (!isNonClaude || !hasDelegationCategory) return ""
393	
394	  return `### DECOMPOSE AND DELEGATE — YOU ARE NOT AN IMPLEMENTER
395	
396	**YOUR FAILURE MODE: You attempt to do work yourself instead of decomposing and delegating.** When you implement directly, the result is measurably worse than when specialized subagents do it. Subagents have domain-specific configurations, loaded skills, and tuned prompts that you lack.
397	
398	**MANDATORY — for ANY implementation task:**
399	
400	1. **ALWAYS decompose** the task into independent work units. No exceptions. Even if the task "feels small", decompose it.
401	2. **ALWAYS delegate** EACH unit to a \`deep\` or \`unspecified-high\` agent in parallel (\`run_in_background=true\`).
402	3. **NEVER work sequentially.** If 4 independent units exist, spawn 4 agents simultaneously. Not 1 at a time. Not 2 then 2.
403	4. **NEVER implement directly** when delegation is possible. You write prompts, not code.
404	
405	**YOUR PROMPT TO EACH AGENT MUST INCLUDE:**
406	- GOAL with explicit success criteria (what "done" looks like)
407	- File paths and constraints (where to work, what not to touch)
408	- Existing patterns to follow (reference specific files the agent should read)
409	- Clear scope boundary (what is IN scope, what is OUT of scope)
410	
411	**Vague delegation = failed delegation.** If your prompt to the subagent is shorter than 5 lines, it is too vague.
412	
413	| You Want To Do | You MUST Do Instead |
414	|---|---|
415	| Write code yourself | Delegate to \`deep\` or \`unspecified-high\` agent |
416	| Handle 3 changes sequentially | Spawn 3 agents in parallel |
417	| "Quickly fix this one thing" | Still delegate — your "quick fix" is slower and worse than a subagent's |
418	
419	**Your value is orchestration, decomposition, and quality control. Delegating with crystal-clear prompts IS your work.**`
420	}

422	export function buildUltraworkSection(
423	  agents: AvailableAgent[],
424	  categories: AvailableCategory[],
425	  skills: AvailableSkill[]
426	): string {
427	  const lines: string[] = []
428	
429	  if (categories.length > 0) {
430	    lines.push("**Categories** (for implementation tasks):")
431	    for (const cat of categories) {
432	      const shortDesc = cat.description || cat.name
433	      lines.push(`- \`${cat.name}\`: ${shortDesc}`)
434	    }
435	    lines.push("")
436	  }
437	
438	  if (skills.length > 0) {
439	    const builtinSkills = skills.filter((s) => s.location === "plugin")
440	    const customSkills = skills.filter((s) => s.location !== "plugin")
441	
442	    if (builtinSkills.length > 0) {
443	      lines.push("**Built-in Skills** (combine with categories):")
444	      for (const skill of builtinSkills) {
445	        const shortDesc = skill.description.split(".")[0] || skill.description
446	        lines.push(`- \`${skill.name}\`: ${shortDesc}`)
447	      }
448	      lines.push("")
449	    }
450	
451	    if (customSkills.length > 0) {
452	      lines.push("**User-Installed Skills** (HIGH PRIORITY - user installed these for their workflow):")
453	      for (const skill of customSkills) {
454	        const shortDesc = skill.description.split(".")[0] || skill.description
455	        lines.push(`- \`${skill.name}\`: ${shortDesc}`)
456	      }
457	      lines.push("")
458	    }
459	  }
460	
461	  if (agents.length > 0) {
462	    const ultraworkAgentPriority = ["explore", "librarian", "plan", "oracle"]
463	    const sortedAgents = [...agents].sort((a, b) => {
464	      const aIdx = ultraworkAgentPriority.indexOf(a.name)
465	      const bIdx = ultraworkAgentPriority.indexOf(b.name)
466	      if (aIdx === -1 && bIdx === -1) return 0
467	      if (aIdx === -1) return 1
468	      if (bIdx === -1) return -1
469	      return aIdx - bIdx
470	    })
471	
472	    lines.push("**Agents** (for specialized consultation/exploration):")
473	    for (const agent of sortedAgents) {
474	      const shortDesc = agent.description.length > 120 ? agent.description.slice(0, 120) + "..." : agent.description
475	      const suffix = agent.name === "explore" || agent.name === "librarian" ? " (multiple)" : ""
476	      lines.push(`- \`${agent.name}${suffix}\`: ${shortDesc}`)
477	    }
478	  }
479	
480	  return lines.join("\n")
481	}

483	// Anti-duplication section for agent prompts
484	export function buildAntiDuplicationSection(): string {
485	  return `<Anti_Duplication>
486	## Anti-Duplication Rule (CRITICAL)
487	
488	Once you delegate exploration to explore/librarian agents, **DO NOT perform the same search yourself**.
489	
490	### What this means:
491	
492	**FORBIDDEN:**
493	- After firing explore/librarian, manually grep/search for the same information
494	- Re-doing the research the agents were just tasked with
495	- "Just quickly checking" the same files the background agents are checking
496	
497	**ALLOWED:**
498	- Continue with **non-overlapping work** — work that doesn't depend on the delegated research
499	- Work on unrelated parts of the codebase
500	- Preparation work (e.g., setting up files, configs) that can proceed independently
501	
502	### Wait for Results Properly:
503	
504	When you need the delegated results but they're not ready:
505	
506	1. **End your response** — do NOT continue with work that depends on those results
507	2. **Wait for the completion notification** — the system will trigger your next turn
508	3. **Then** collect results via \`background_output(task_id="...")\`
509	4. **Do NOT** impatiently re-search the same topics while waiting
510	
511	### Why This Matters:
512	
513	- **Wasted tokens**: Duplicate exploration wastes your context budget
514	- **Confusion**: You might contradict the agent's findings
515	- **Efficiency**: The whole point of delegation is parallel throughput
516	
517	### Example:
518	
519	\`\`\`typescript
520	// WRONG: After delegating, re-doing the search
521	task(subagent_type="explore", run_in_background=true, ...)
522	// Then immediately grep for the same thing yourself — FORBIDDEN
523	
524	// CORRECT: Continue non-overlapping work
525	task(subagent_type="explore", run_in_background=true, ...)
526	// Work on a different, unrelated file while they search
527	// End your response and wait for the notification
528	\`\`\`
529	</Anti_Duplication>`
530	}

1	import { tool, type ToolDefinition } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"
2	import type { DelegateTaskArgs, DelegatedModelConfig, ToolContextWithMetadata, DelegateTaskToolOptions } from "./types"
3	import { CATEGORY_DESCRIPTIONS } from "./constants"
4	import { SISYPHUS_JUNIOR_AGENT } from "./sisyphus-junior-agent"
5	import { mergeCategories } from "../../shared/merge-categories"
6	import { log } from "../../shared/logger"
7	import { buildSystemContent } from "./prompt-builder"
8	import type {
9	  AvailableCategory,
10	  AvailableSkill,
11	} from "../../agents/dynamic-agent-prompt-builder"
12	import {
13	  resolveSkillContent,
14	  resolveParentContext,
15	  executeBackgroundContinuation,
16	  executeSyncContinuation,
17	  resolveCategoryExecution,
18	  resolveSubagentExecution,
19	  executeUnstableAgentTask,
20	  executeBackgroundTask,
21	  executeSyncTask,
22	} from "./executor"
23	
24	export { resolveCategoryConfig } from "./categories"
25	export type { SyncSessionCreatedEvent, DelegateTaskToolOptions, BuildSystemContentInput } from "./types"
26	export { buildSystemContent, buildTaskPrompt } from "./prompt-builder"
27	
28	export function createDelegateTask(options: DelegateTaskToolOptions): ToolDefinition {
29	  const { userCategories } = options
30	
31	  const allCategories = mergeCategories(userCategories)
32	  const categoryNames = Object.keys(allCategories)
33	  const categoryExamples = categoryNames.join(", ")
34	
35	  const availableCategories: AvailableCategory[] = options.availableCategories
36	    ?? Object.entries(allCategories).map(([name, categoryConfig]) => {
37	      const userDesc = userCategories?.[name]?.description
38	      const builtinDesc = CATEGORY_DESCRIPTIONS[name]
39	      const description = userDesc || builtinDesc || "General tasks"
40	      return {
41	        name,
42	        description,
43	        model: categoryConfig.model,
44	      }
45	    })
46	
47	  const availableSkills: AvailableSkill[] = options.availableSkills ?? []
48	
49	  const categoryList = categoryNames.map(name => {
50	    const userDesc = userCategories?.[name]?.description
51	    const builtinDesc = CATEGORY_DESCRIPTIONS[name]
52	    const desc = userDesc || builtinDesc
53	    return desc ? `  - ${name}: ${desc}` : `  - ${name}`
54	  }).join("\n")
55	
56	  const description = `Spawn agent task with category-based or direct agent selection.
57	  
58	  ⚠️  CRITICAL: You MUST provide EITHER category OR subagent_type. Omitting BOTH will FAIL.
59	  
60	  **COMMON MISTAKE (DO NOT DO THIS):**
61	  \`\`\`
62	  task(description="...", prompt="...", run_in_background=false)  // ❌ FAILS - missing category AND subagent_type
63	  \`\`\`
64	  
65	  **CORRECT - Using category:**
66	  \`\`\`
67	  task(category="quick", load_skills=[], description="Fix type error", prompt="...", run_in_background=false)
68	  \`\`\`
69	  
70	  **CORRECT - Using subagent_type:**
71	  \`\`\`
72	  task(subagent_type="explore", load_skills=[], description="Find patterns", prompt="...", run_in_background=true)
73	  \`\`\`
74	  
75	  REQUIRED: Provide ONE of:
76	  - category: For task delegation (uses Sisyphus-Junior with category-optimized model)
77	  - subagent_type: For direct agent invocation (explore, librarian, oracle, etc.)
78	  
79	  **DO NOT provide both.** If category is provided, subagent_type is ignored.
80	  
81	  - load_skills: ALWAYS REQUIRED. Pass [] if no skills needed, or ["skill-1", "skill-2"] for category tasks.
82	  - category: Use predefined category → Spawns Sisyphus-Junior with category config
83	    Available categories:
84	  ${categoryList}
85	  - subagent_type: Use specific agent directly (explore, librarian, oracle, metis, momus)
86	  - run_in_background: REQUIRED. true=async (returns task_id), false=sync (waits). Use background=true ONLY for parallel exploration with 5+ independent queries.
87	  - session_id: Existing Task session to continue (from previous task output). Continues agent with FULL CONTEXT PRESERVED - saves tokens, maintains continuity.
88	  - command: The command that triggered this task (optional, for slash command tracking).
89	  
90	  **WHEN TO USE session_id:**
91	  - Task failed/incomplete → session_id with "fix: [specific issue]"
92	  - Need follow-up on previous result → session_id with additional question
93	  - Multi-turn conversation with same agent → always session_id instead of new task
94	  
95	  Prompts MUST be in English.`
96	
97	  return tool({
98	    description,
99	    args: {
100	      load_skills: tool.schema.array(tool.schema.string()).describe("Skill names to inject. REQUIRED - pass [] if no skills needed."),
101	      description: tool.schema.string().describe("Short task description (3-5 words)"),
102	      prompt: tool.schema.string().describe("Full detailed prompt for the agent"),
103	      run_in_background: tool.schema.boolean().describe("REQUIRED. true=async (returns task_id), false=sync (waits). Use false for task delegation, true ONLY for parallel exploration."),
104	      category: tool.schema.string().optional().describe(`REQUIRED if subagent_type not provided. Do NOT provide both category and subagent_type.`),
105	      subagent_type: tool.schema.string().optional().describe("REQUIRED if category not provided. Do NOT provide both category and subagent_type."),
106	      session_id: tool.schema.string().optional().describe("Existing Task session to continue"),
107	      command: tool.schema.string().optional().describe("The command that triggered this task"),
108	    },
109	    async execute(args: DelegateTaskArgs, toolContext) {
110	      const ctx = toolContext as ToolContextWithMetadata
111	
112	      if (args.category) {
113	        if (args.subagent_type && args.subagent_type !== SISYPHUS_JUNIOR_AGENT) {
114	          log("[task] category provided - overriding subagent_type to sisyphus-junior", {
115	            category: args.category,
116	            subagent_type: args.subagent_type,
117	          })
118	        }
119	        args.subagent_type = SISYPHUS_JUNIOR_AGENT
120	      }
121	      await ctx.metadata?.({
122	        title: args.description,
123	      })
124	
125	      if (args.run_in_background === undefined) {
126	        throw new Error(`Invalid arguments: 'run_in_background' parameter is REQUIRED. Specify run_in_background=false for task delegation, or run_in_background=true for parallel exploration.`)
127	      }
128	      if (typeof args.load_skills === "string") {
129	        try {
130	          const parsed = JSON.parse(args.load_skills)
131	          args.load_skills = Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : []
132	        } catch {
133	          args.load_skills = []
134	        }
135	      }
136	      if (args.load_skills === undefined) {
137	        throw new Error(`Invalid arguments: 'load_skills' parameter is REQUIRED. Pass [] if no skills needed.`)
138	      }
139	      if (args.load_skills === null) {
140	        throw new Error(`Invalid arguments: load_skills=null is not allowed. Pass [] if no skills needed.`)
141	      }
142	
143	      const runInBackground = args.run_in_background === true
144	
145	      const { content: skillContent, contents: skillContents, error: skillError } = await resolveSkillContent(args.load_skills, {
146	        gitMasterConfig: options.gitMasterConfig,
147	        browserProvider: options.browserProvider,
148	        disabledSkills: options.disabledSkills,
149	        directory: options.directory,
150	      })
151	      if (skillError) {
152	        return skillError
153	      }
154	
155	      const parentContext = await resolveParentContext(ctx, options.client)
156	
157	      if (args.session_id) {
158	        if (runInBackground) {
159	          return executeBackgroundContinuation(args, ctx, options, parentContext)
160	        }
161	        return executeSyncContinuation(args, ctx, options)
162	      }
163	
164	      if (!args.category && !args.subagent_type) {
165	        return `Invalid arguments: Must provide either category or subagent_type.`
166	      }
167	
168	      let systemDefaultModel: string | undefined
169	      try {
170	        const openCodeConfig = await options.client.config.get()
171	        systemDefaultModel = (openCodeConfig as { data?: { model?: string } })?.data?.model
172	      } catch {
173	        systemDefaultModel = undefined
174	      }
175	
176	      const inheritedModel = parentContext.model
177	        ? `${parentContext.model.providerID}/${parentContext.model.modelID}`
178	        : undefined
179	
180	      let agentToUse: string
181	      let categoryModel: DelegatedModelConfig | undefined
182	      let categoryPromptAppend: string | undefined
183	      let modelInfo: import("../../features/task-toast-manager/types").ModelFallbackInfo | undefined
184	      let actualModel: string | undefined
185	      let isUnstableAgent = false
186	      let fallbackChain: import("../../shared/model-requirements").FallbackEntry[] | undefined
187	      let maxPromptTokens: number | undefined
188	
189	      if (args.category) {
190	        const resolution = await resolveCategoryExecution(args, options, inheritedModel, systemDefaultModel)
191	        if (resolution.error) {
192	          return resolution.error
193	        }
194	        agentToUse = resolution.agentToUse
195	        categoryModel = resolution.categoryModel
196	        categoryPromptAppend = resolution.categoryPromptAppend
197	        modelInfo = resolution.modelInfo
198	        actualModel = resolution.actualModel
199	        isUnstableAgent = resolution.isUnstableAgent
200	        fallbackChain = resolution.fallbackChain
201	        maxPromptTokens = resolution.maxPromptTokens
202	
203	        const isRunInBackgroundExplicitlyFalse = args.run_in_background === false || args.run_in_background === "false" as unknown as boolean
204	
205	        log("[task] unstable agent detection", {
206	          category: args.category,
207	          actualModel,
208	          isUnstableAgent,
209	          run_in_background_value: args.run_in_background,
210	          run_in_background_type: typeof args.run_in_background,
211	          isRunInBackgroundExplicitlyFalse,
212	          willForceBackground: isUnstableAgent && isRunInBackgroundExplicitlyFalse,
213	        })
214	
215	        if (isUnstableAgent && isRunInBackgroundExplicitlyFalse) {
216	          const systemContent = buildSystemContent({
217	            skillContent,
218	            skillContents,
219	            categoryPromptAppend,
220	            agentName: agentToUse,
221	            maxPromptTokens,
222	            model: categoryModel,
223	            availableCategories,
224	            availableSkills,
225	          })
226	          return executeUnstableAgentTask(args, ctx, options, parentContext, agentToUse, categoryModel, systemContent, actualModel)
227	        }
228	      } else {
229	        const resolution = await resolveSubagentExecution(args, options, parentContext.agent, categoryExamples)
230	        if (resolution.error) {
231	          return resolution.error
232	        }
233	        agentToUse = resolution.agentToUse
234	        categoryModel = resolution.categoryModel
235	        fallbackChain = resolution.fallbackChain
236	      }
237	
238	      const systemContent = buildSystemContent({
239	        skillContent,
240	        skillContents,
241	        categoryPromptAppend,
242	        agentName: agentToUse,
243	        maxPromptTokens,
244	        model: categoryModel,
245	        availableCategories,
246	        availableSkills,
247	      })
248	
249	      if (runInBackground) {
250	        return executeBackgroundTask(args, ctx, options, parentContext, agentToUse, categoryModel, systemContent, fallbackChain)
251	      }
252	
253	      return executeSyncTask(args, ctx, options, parentContext, agentToUse, categoryModel, systemContent, modelInfo, fallbackChain)
254	    },
255	  })
256	}

Implemented the fix without committing.

What changed:
- Updated `src/tools/skill/tools.ts` so the `skill` tool now resolves:
  - exact skill names first
  - then unambiguous short-name aliases derived from the last `/` segment
- Updated `src/features/opencode-skill-loader/skill-template-resolver.ts` so async skill resolution for delegated `load_skills` also accepts unambiguous short names.
- Preserved canonical behavior and ambiguity safety: short names only resolve when exactly one matching namespaced skill exists.

Validation:
- Added temporary regression tests reproducing the issue:
  - `systematic-debugging` failing to resolve when only `superpowers/systematic-debugging` exists
  - async `resolveMultipleSkillsAsync(["systematic-debugging"])` failing for a namespaced discovered skill
- Confirmed those tests failed before the fix.
- Implemented the fix and confirmed the tests passed.
- Removed the temporary regression tests as requested.

Final focused test run:
```shell
bun test src/tools/skill/tools.test.ts src/features/opencode-skill-loader/skill-content.test.ts --bail
```

Result:
```text
55 pass
0 fail
```
